title: BlackBerry Q2 2024 Earnings Preview and Analysis
text: BlackBerry Q2 2024 earnings were expected to show strong performance in QNX and Secure Communications divisions. The company faced potential headwinds in the automotive sector due to industry strike action, semiconductor supply challenges, and transition to software-defined vehicles. BlackBerry revised its IoT revenue outlook to $225-240 million, representing 9-17% year-over-year growth. Total company gross margin was projected at 65%. Operating expenses were expected at $114 million. Non-GAAP net loss was estimated at $23 million. The company's cybersecurity revenue continued to show resilience with strong ARR momentum. The market was watching for evidence of monetization of the QNX technology backlog and expansion beyond automotive into robotics and industrial IoT. BlackBerry had beaten EPS estimates in a majority of recent quarters, supporting investor confidence in management execution.
